livedEvent.summaryZh: She was only tidying up for a second, then paused because of planner.
livedEvent.hookWhyInterestingZh: It is tiny, but it feels like the sort of fragment a real day would leave behind.
causalChainZh: She was only moving through an ordinary routine.
One specific detail briefly pulled her attention sideways.
The feeling did not grow into drama; it just settled for a moment.

inferredDetailsZh: The detail was minor in itself, but today it happened to register.

resonanceOnlyZh: 下午三点，书店里很安静。
阳光透过窗，在书页上投下淡淡的影子。
空气里有旧纸和油墨的味道。

characterInterior.before: She was only trying to move through the moment normally.
characterInterior.after: A tiny detail made her pause internally for half a beat.
characterInterior.whyPostToday: One tiny beat briefly felt worth keeping.

externalEventFacts: A temporary stationery and notebook display is set near the quiet bookstore aisle entrance.
Weekend browsing traffic is slightly denser around the entrance shelves than on an ordinary weekday.

postIntent.characterPresenceGuidanceEn: the protagonist should be clearly readable in the frame through face, posture, or fuller-body action while the lived event stays primary
postIntent.whyShareableZh: It is concrete and quiet at the same time, so it can become a believable post.

captureIntent.summaryEn: observational camera relation, medium distance, partial body coverage, glimpse face readability, balanced environment weight
guidanceEn: the frame should feel witnessed from the outside, not automatically self-held
a brief or partial face glimpse is enough; do not force portrait dominance
partial body coverage is enough when it keeps the moment concrete
medium distance is a stable everyday framing for ordinary daily-life moments
balance the protagonist and environment so they support the same lived beat together
